steps: Factor \(x\) from the left side of \(-3x + 18px = 90\) to get \(x(-3 + 18p) = 90\).

For a linear equation \(ax = c\) with \(c \neq 0\) to have no solution, the coefficient \(a\) must equal 0 (producing \(0 = 90\), a contradiction). Since \(90 \neq 0\), we need \(-3 + 18p = 0\), so \(18p = 3\) and \(p = \frac{1}{6}\).

Test all four choices:
  • Choice A — '5': \(p = 5\) gives coefficient \(-3 + 18(5) = 87 eq 0\), so \(x = rac{90}{87}\) — exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice B — '0': \(p = 0\) gives coefficient \(-3 + 0 = -3 eq 0\), so \(x = rac{90}{-3} = -30\) — exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice C — '\( rac{5}{3}\)': \(p = rac{5}{3}\) gives coefficient \(-3 + 18 \cdot rac{5}{3} = -3 + 30 = 27 eq 0\) — exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice D — '\( rac{1}{6}\)': \(p = rac{1}{6}\) gives coefficient \(-3 + 18 \cdot rac{1}{6} = -3 + 3 = 0\), producing \(0 = 90\) — a contradiction with no solution. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
